title: sempervivum tectorum subsp. lainzii
intro: Sempervivum tectorum subsp. lainzii, also known as Lainz's houseleek, is a succulent plant species within the genus Sempervivum, characterized by its rosette-shaped leaves and ability to thrive in arid environments. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lainz's houseleek include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, ointments, or salves, which are applied topically to benefit from its medicinal properties.
